mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid unemployment spikes signal deteriorating labor demand and impending consumption contraction, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and equity multiples.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high and low joint growth quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained regime signals pricing power and margin expansion, directly supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Direction flips to negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% YoY, or forward returns show no statistical difference across production deciles.

mechanism_narrative: Disinflation without rate cuts creates a Goldilocks environment that lifts equity multiples by improving real yields and corporate profit margins simultaneously.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0% YoY, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect sustained consumer spending, which directly supports S&P 500 earnings and drives positive forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns show no statistical difference between high and low RSAFS growth periods (p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong non-farm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment confirms labor market resilience, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns decline when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k/month for three consecutive months, or the joint signal fails to predict returns above 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-1.5% annualized GDP growth signals economic contraction, triggering earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where GDP growth remains below 1.5% for two consecutive quarters, invalidating the contraction signal.
